behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the key-derivation logic itself (deterministic normalization/serialization/hash), which can be exercised in one function/object with no I/O. Rejected Integration: behavior text is only about mapping query-parameter inputs to keys, not cross-module handoff. No real OS/network/subprocess (so not System), no model judgment (not Agentic), no multi-agent chain (not Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the key generator directly with canonicalized parameter sets: same semantic params in different orders must produce identical keys; deliberately different params (including edge cases like null/empty/list values) must produce different keys across a large corpus/property test.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic rule evaluation (category + config -> TTL) and can fail entirely within one computation boundary. Rejected Integration: behavior text does not require config loading pipeline or DB interaction, only correct computation from configured rule values. No real infrastructure, no AI output dependency, no multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test `computeTTL(category, rules)` with table-driven cases (user_profiles=300s, product_catalog=3600s, unknown/default category behavior, boundary values). Assert exact TTL outputs and units.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: Real failure typically appears at module boundaries: memory-usage monitor/metric + config + strategy selector wiring. Unit tests of selector logic alone can miss miswired thresholds or stale metric propagation. Integration brake: (a) no required real OS memory metric in behavior text (threshold can be simulated in-process), so System not required; (b) no AI dependency; (c) no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test cache manager with in-process modules wired together: feed memory-usage readings below/at/above threshold and verify active strategy transitions exactly per config (LRU before threshold, LFU after threshold, including hysteresis/edge condition if defined).

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real PostgreSQL write effects triggering cache invalidation; failures often depend on real DB semantics (transactions, commit timing, trigger/NOTIFY/change-feed behavior) that mocks can hide. Rejected Integration: concrete text says "write to the underlying PostgreSQL table," which keeps the real DB boundary in scope. Deterministic contract handling, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: System test with real PostgreSQL instance and real cache layer: prewarm cache for rows, execute INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on target table, commit, then assert matching cache keys are invalidated while unrelated keys remain. Include timing/assertion window consistent with invalidation mechanism.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Minimum realistic failure boundary is cache-state bookkeeping plus policy module interaction (recency/frequency counters updated by access path, then consumed by eviction). Pure unit tests of policy ranking can miss wiring bugs in state updates. Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required, no AI/ML dependency, no multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test cache store with each policy enabled separately: construct controlled access patterns, fill to capacity, add one more entry, and assert evicted key matches expected LRU/LFU victim based on actual recorded recency/frequency from the integrated access path.
